channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: What to know about the plastic pollution treaty talks that have concluded in South Korea
item.description: BUSAN, South Korea (AP) — Negotiations on a treaty to end plastic pollution have concluded in Busan, South Korea without reaching an agreement.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ireland's incumbent parties look likely to hang on to power after a fractured election
item.description: DUBLIN (AP) — Ireland’s two long-dominant center-right parties looked likely to form a new government as results came in Sunday from a fractured national election, though with a reduced vote share and complex coalition negotiations ahead.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PETER MORRISON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Canada's ambassador says Trudeau told Trump the Canadian border can't be compared to Mexico's
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's ambassador to the United States said Sunday that Prime Minister Justin Trudeau was successful in getting President-elect Donald Trump and key Cabinet nominees to understand that lumping Canada in with Mexico over the flow of drugs and migrants into the U.S. is unfair.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Syria launches counterattacks in an attempt to halt insurgency, as Iran's top diplomat meets Assad
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Syrian military rushed reinforcements to the northwest and launched airstrikes Sunday in an attempt to push back insurgents who seized the country’s largest city of Aleppo, as Iran pledged to help the government counter the surprise offensive.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Countries deadlock on UN treaty on plastic pollution
item.description: Countries have deadlocked on a United Nations treaty on plastic pollution, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan's president meets Hawaii's governor and members of Congress in a visit condemned by China
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — On a two-day visit to Hawaii, Taiwan’s president Lai Ching-te met with the state's governor and congressional representatives as part of a Pacific island tour that has already triggered criticism from Beijing. On Sunday, China’s Foreign Ministry said it “strongly condemned” U.S.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Over 40 people hospitalized in Georgia during protests over the suspension of EU talks
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — A third night of protests in the Georgian capital against the government’s decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union left 44 people hospitalized, officials said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Former NATO chief says Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire a 'direct result' of incoming Trump admin
item.description: James Stavridis, former NATO supreme allied commander, said he believes the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire agreement was successful in part because of President-elect Trump’s impending return to power. “Iran [is] very vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: After entering Aleppo, Syrian insurgents advance to a nearby province. Assad says he'll defeat them
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Thousands of Syrian insurgents took over most of Aleppo on Saturday, establishing positions in the country's largest city and controlling its airport before expanding their shock offensive to a nearby province.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas releases new hostage video of American-Israeli Edan Alexander
item.description: Hamas released a propaganda video Saturday showing the 20-year-old Alexander alive, marking his first proof of life since being kidnapped.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Counting is underway in Ireland's election as 3 parties battle for top place
item.description: DUBLIN (AP) — A marathon vote-counting exercise was underway Saturday in Ireland’s national election after an exit poll suggested that the contest is a close-fought race among the country’s three largest political parties.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PETER MORRISON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An Israeli strike in Gaza kills World Central Kitchen workers. Israel says 1 was an Oct. 7 attacker
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike on a car in the Gaza Strip on Saturday killed five people, including employees of World Central Kitchen.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, TIA GOLDENBERG and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Brazilian police say Bolsonaro plotted a coup to stay in office
item.description: Brazil's Federal Police in November formally accused former President Jair Bolsonaro and more than 30 other people of attempting a coup to keep him in office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy says NATO offer for Ukraine-controlled territory could end 'hot stage' of war
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An offer of NATO membership to territory under Kyiv’s control would end “the hot stage of the war” in Ukraine, but any proposal to join the military alliance should be extended to all parts of the country that fall under internationally recognized borders, President Volodymyr Ze
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump lauds 'productive' meeting with Trudeau amid tension over tariffs
item.description: President-elect Trump confirmed his Friday meeting with Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, calling the conversation "productive.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: Israeli military says Hezbollah is smuggling weapons into Lebanon during ceasefire
item.description: Hezbollah terrorists reportedly are smuggling weapons across Lebanon's borders despite an ongoing ceasefire with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelensky suggests NATO membership could halt 'hot phase’ of war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky suggested this week that NATO welcoming Ukraine into the alliance could halt the “hot stage” of the war, while Russia would temporarily keep the territory it overtook.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan's president arrives in Hawaii for a 2-day stop in the US as part of South Pacific visit
item.description: Taiwan’s President Lai Ching-te has arrived in Hawaii for a two-day transit in the U.S. as part of a trip to the South Pacific.
item.dc:creator: Taijing Wu And Huizhong Wu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In Bolivia's scrappy highlands, proud Indigenous Cholas take the runway by storm
item.description: VIACHA, Bolivia (AP) — In the huddled markets, sprawling farms and pulsing parties of Viacha, a town southeast of Bolivia’s capital, it's typical for women to sport bowler hats, tiered skirts and fringed shawls.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and JUAN KARITA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea's Kim vows steadfast support for Russia’s war in Ukraine
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed his country will “invariably support” Russia’s war in Ukraine as he met Russia's defense chief, the North’s state media reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Imprisoned Kremlin critic convicted again, receives 3-year sentence for opposing war in Ukraine
item.description: Imprisoned Kremlin critic Alexei Gorinov was convicted again on Friday and sentenced to three years in prison for opposing Russia's ongoing war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Romania's parliamentary vote risks being overshadowed by presidential race chaos
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Hundreds of protesters gathered in Bucharest after a far-right populist unexpectedly won the first round of a presidential race, plunging Romania into turmoil just days before the country holds parliamentary elections. “Better to be dead than a fascist,” one placard reads.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump wants a Gaza ceasefire deal 'now,' Graham says
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) said that President-elect Trump wants to reach a ceasefire and hostage deal before inauguration day on Jan. 20, Axios reported on Friday. "Trump is more determined than ever to release the hostages and supports a ceasefire that includes a hostage deal.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Georgian protesters clash with police for a second night after EU talks are suspended
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Thousands of demonstrators protesting the Georgian government's decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union rallied outside the parliament and clashed with police for a second straight night on Friday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about shock offensive on Syria's largest city
item.description: Syrian rebels against the country's President Bashar Al-Assad advanced toward one of the largest cities in the country — the first time since 2016 — following a shock offensive launched by insurgents on Wednesday, the Associated Press reported on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Enraged passenger unloads flurry of punches on airport worker, wild video shows
item.description: A furious airline passenger was captured on dramatic video unleashing a flurry of punches on an airport worker over an apparent check-in delay dispute.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: 2 children and a woman crushed to death outside Gaza bakery amid food shortage
item.description: Two children and a woman were crushed to death Friday as a crowd of Palestinians pushed to get bread at a bakery in the Gaza Strip amid a worsening food crisis in the war-ravaged territory, medical officials said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas attacker opens fire at Israeli bus in West Bank, injuring at least 8: report
item.description: Hamas is claiming responsibility Friday for an attack on a bus near an Israeli settlement in the West Bank, which has left at least eight injured, reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Customers at this Starbucks can sip coffee and observe a quiet North Korean village
item.description: Coffee drinkers can sip their beverages and view a quiet North Korean mountain village from a new Starbucks at a South Korean border observatory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: British lawmakers give initial approval to a bill to allow terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British lawmakers gave initial approval on Friday to a bill to help terminally ill adults end their lives in England and Wales, following an impassioned debate that saw people sharing personal stories of loss and suffering.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nigeria confirms that at least 27 people died and more than 100 are missing after boat capsized
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — At least 27 people died and more than 100, mostly women, were missing on Friday, after a boat transporting them to a food market capsized along the River Niger in northern Nigeria, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: First look at Notre Dame Cathedral's restored interior five years after devastating fire
item.description: Images and videos reveal the restored interior of the Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris five years after a massive fire caused extensive damage, including a collapsed roof.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Powerful Mexican cartel accused of forced labor, human trafficking
item.description: The Gulf Cartel is a long-standing, powerful drug trafficking organization that operates throughout Tamaulipas.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: China releases 3 Americans in prisoner swap
item.description: Mark Swidan, Kai Li and John Leung were freed after years of talks between U.S. and Chinese officials.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: 2 companies cut ties with Conor McGregor
item.description: Proximo Spirits, the owner of Irish whiskey brand Proper No. 12, will no longer feature McGregor’s name.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
